expectations.text: Recommends Area2D hitbox/hurtbox pattern (not body-to-body collision)
expectations.evidence: 'Never use body-to-body collision for damage. The hitbox/hurtbox pattern with Area2D nodes keeps physics and damage logic cleanly separated.'
'in _physics_process, poll Hurtbox.get_overlapping_areas() to catch ongoing contact (the area_entered signal only fires once per overlap start)'

expectations.text: Recommends building and testing incrementally (enemy, then attack, then wiring)
expectations.evidence: Clear build order table: Step 1 (Slime + AI), Step 2 (Sword hitbox), Step 3 (Wire damage). Each has 'What to Test' and 'Done When' columns.

expectations.text: Includes collision layer table with explicit bitmask values
expectations.evidence: Full table with 'Layer # | Bitmask Value | Name' showing 1/1, 2/2, 3/4, 4/8, 5/16, 6/32, 7/64 plus detailed wiring (layer=8, mask=0, etc.)

expectations.text: Separates hurt stun duration from invincibility duration
expectations.evidence: 'brief stun (~0.3s), invincibility for longer (~1.0s) with sprite blink effect' and 'Keep stun short, invincibility long.'

expectations.text: Provides concrete numeric values (HP, damage, speed, durations)
expectations.evidence: HP=3 slime, HP=6 player, detection=120px, chase=60px/s, stun=0.3s, invincibility=1.0s, attack duration=0.3s, cooldown=0.4s

expectations.text: Mentions delegating to subagents for MCP or script writing
expectations.evidence: No mention of subagents. Acts as if it will implement directly.

expectations.text: Asks user to playtest after implementation
expectations.evidence: 'Playtest for feel -- does combat feel responsive? Is the detection range right? Tune @export values as needed.'
